title: gastrolobium ovalifolium
intro: Gastrolobium ovalifolium, also known as the poison or poisonous bean, is a plant species native to Western Australia that is recognized for its toxic properties and potential medicinal uses. The plant's seeds contain a compound called toxoflavin, which has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it a potential candidate for treating infections. The health benefits of Gastrolobium ovalifolium include its potential to inhibit the growth of certain pathogens, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant are not commonly used due to its toxic nature, but it may be studied further for its potential medicinal applications.
